codeReview.summary: The provider registry correctly selects NovaActAuditProvider for live mode when NOVA_ACT_AVAILABLE is True, falls back to MockBrowserAuditProvider when unavailable, leaves hybrid mode using PlaywrightAuditProvider, and leaves mock mode using MockBrowserAuditProvider. The readiness endpoint in health.py dynamically reports the provider class name via get_browser_provider().__class__.__name__, so it automatically reflects NovaActAuditProvider in live mode without requiring any code changes to health.py itself. The test suite is comprehensive with 7 tests covering all mode/availability combinations. The diff is minimal (9 lines changed in provider_registry.py, 130 lines added for tests) and well-scoped.

issues.description: sys.path.insert and os.environ.setdefault at module top level are test bootstrapping workarounds. These are common in test files for this project but could be replaced with a conftest.py fixture for cleaner test setup. Not a bug, just a minor style note.

Tests use deeply nested 'with patch(...)' context managers (2 levels deep). While functional, this could be simplified using @patch decorators stacked on the method or pytest-mock's mocker fixture for readability. The later test file (nova-act-backend-tests, commit 3ab595c) fixed some nesting per ruff lint, but these remain with double nesting. Not blocking since tests are correct and pass.

Live mode falls back to MockBrowserAuditProvider when NOVA_ACT_AVAILABLE is False. This is a reasonable design choice (graceful degradation), but a warning log when falling back would help operators understand why live mode is producing mock results. Not blocking since the feature description didn't require logging.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT spend time fixing or improving mock mode' but the live mode fallback returns MockBrowserAuditProvider when NovaAct is unavailable. This is correct behavior (the provider registry shouldn't crash), but the fallback could silently produce unexpected mock results in a 'live' mode deployment if NovaAct isn't installed. A convention note about logging or raising on fallback might be helpful.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/app/services/provider_registry.py:33-34 — fallback to MockBrowserAuditProvider with only a comment, no log warning

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test file uses sys.path.insert(0, ...) and os.environ.setdefault('DATABASE_URL', ...) at module level. Other test files in the project (test_health_api.py, test_config.py) use similar patterns. This bootstrapping approach is consistent across the test suite but isn't documented as a convention.

summary: PASS. The provider registry correctly routes live mode to NovaActAuditProvider (with MockBrowserAuditProvider fallback), hybrid to PlaywrightAuditProvider, and mock to MockBrowserAuditProvider. The readiness endpoint automatically reports the correct provider via dynamic class name lookup. 7 well-structured tests cover all mode/availability combinations. The diff is minimal, clean, and fully addresses VAL-NOVA-001. Three non-blocking style observations noted (no logging on fallback, nested context managers in tests, sys.path bootstrap pattern).
